mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns exceed negative returns by more than 15% in high-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or the signal loses statistical significance at the 90% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust household balance sheets and consumer confidence, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by more than 2% annually over rolling 4-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term momentum buying and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive policy rates compresses real corporate margins and increases borrowing costs, creating a dual pressure that forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power with a t-statistic below 1.5, or forward returns turn positive when both thresholds are breached.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and supports aggregate consumption, which directly lifts corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.01, or payroll growth fails to correlate with equity returns during structural employment shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding a 2.0% annualized pace confirms macroeconomic expansion and validates corporate revenue projections, which reduces recession risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences or fails to beat a risk-free benchmark over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and consumer prices signal supply-side bottlenecks and cost-push inflation, which erodes real consumer purchasing power and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a positive predictive accuracy below 50%, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.
